original_thesis: The 'understand the problem before solving it' stack. Jobs-to-be-done frames what actually needs solving; meta-cognitive reflection checks whether you're thinking clearly about it; law-of-large-numbers guards against small-sample errors; scientific method structures the investigation.
corrected_thesis: The 'build an auditable evidence chain' stack. Decomposition breaks the problem; root-cause-analysis traces to the real driver; causal-attribution-resistance prevents accepting convenient explanations; scientific method structures the investigation; critical thinking evaluates the evidence; systems thinking provides whole-picture context; auditability-traceability ensures the reasoning chain can be inspected and verified; time-tested-validation demands that conclusions hold up over repeated observation, not just one analysis.
what_this_stack_defeats: Jumping to solutions, unverifiable reasoning, anecdotal evidence, conclusions that don't survive scrutiny
notes: Original thesis mentioned jobs-to-be-done, meta-cognitive-reflection, LLN — not members. Shares Causal Forensics trunk. Divergence: auditability-traceability + time-tested-validation. These divergence members shift the stack from 'diagnose' to 'diagnose AND prove you're right'. Governance/compliance angle.
